params.description: Horizontal picture graph appears on left. Favorite Pets data with scale of 5. Dogs=20, Cats=15, Fish=10, Birds=5, Lizards=0. Key: each paw symbol = 5 votes.

incorrects.description: Student selected 'In the symbols', confusing bar graph with picture graph format

params.description: Axis numbers highlight to draw attention to where the scale is shown.

steps.text: This is a bar graph, not a picture graph. Bar graphs don't use symbols. Look at the numbers along the side. Those show the scale.

incorrects.description: Student selected 'In the title', misunderstanding where scale information appears

params.description: Axis numbers highlight to draw attention to the scale.

steps.text: The title names what the graph is about. The scale is shown by the numbers along the side. Look at those numbers. What pattern do you see?

incorrects.description: Student answered incorrectly. System models the correct answer.

params.description: Animation traces up the axis, highlighting each number: 0, 5, 10, 15, 20, 25, 30.

steps.text: Let me show you. Watch the axis. The numbers go 0, 5, 10, 15, 20, 25, 30. That's counting by 5s. The axis is where the scale lives in a bar graph. The answer is 'On the axis.' That's how bar graphs show scale of 5.

incorrects.description: Student selected 3 (the number of symbols in the picture graph)

params.description: Slides bar highlights. Axis markings pulse at 5, 10, 15.

steps.text: You counted the symbols in the picture graph. In a bar graph, look where the bar ENDS on the axis. Follow the top of the Slides bar across. What number does it reach?

incorrects.description: Student selected 10 (incorrect axis reading)

params.description: Slides bar highlights. Guideline draws from bar top to axis at 15.

steps.text: Look closely at where the bar ends. The axis counts by 5s: 5, 10, 15. The Slides bar goes past 10. Where does the top of the bar land?

incorrects.description: Student answered incorrectly. System models the correct answer.

params.description: Guideline draws from top of Slides bar across to axis, landing at 15.

steps.text: Let me show you. Follow the top of the Slides bar across to the axis. It ends at 15. That is the bar's height. The bar shows 15 votes, the same as 3 symbols in the picture graph. The height tells you the value.

steps.text: Look at the pattern on the axis. These numbers skip by 5s, not 1s. What number comes after 15?

incorrects.description: Student selected 30: skipped ahead by 10 instead of 5

params.description: Numbers 15, 20, 25 pulse to show the spacing between marks.

steps.text: Each step on this axis goes up by 5, not 10. From 15 to 20 is 5. From 20 to the next mark is another 5. What's 20 plus 5?

params.description: All axis numbers from 0 to 20 pulse to show the consistent interval.

steps.text: This axis counts by 5s, just like you counted symbols. 5, 10, 15, 20, and then what? Keep counting by 5s.

params.description: Axis numbers highlight in sequence: 0, 5, 10, 15, 20, 25, 30, 35, showing the counting by 5s pattern.

steps.text: Let me show you. The axis counts by 5s the whole way up: 0, 5, 10, 15, 20. After 20 comes 25. That's how the scale works. The skip-counting is built into the side of the graph.

steps.text: When we ask how many MORE, we subtract to find the difference. We don't add them together. What's 30 minus 15?

params.description: Visual bracket appears connecting Tacos bar at 30 and Salad bar at 15, showing the gap of 15.

steps.text: Let me show you. Tacos ends at 30 on the axis. Salad ends at 15. To find how many more, I subtract: 30 minus 15 equals 15. The difference between the two bars is 15.

incorrects.description: Student placed incorrect number of symbols three or more times. System models the answer.

params.description: Emma row fills with 4 symbols, Noah row with 5 symbols, Olivia row with 8 symbols.

steps.text: Let me show you. Emma has 20 steps. Count by 5s: 5, 10, 15, 20. That's 4 symbols. Noah has 25: 5, 10, 15, 20, 25. That's 5 symbols. Olivia has 40: 5, 10, 15, 20, 25, 30, 35, 40. That's 8 symbols. Each category gets exactly the number of symbols that matches its value when you count by the scale.

params.description: Emma and Olivia rows highlight.

steps.text: Look at Emma and Olivia. Count the symbols in each row, then skip-count by 5s to find how many books each person read. What's the difference?

incorrects.description: Student selected 40: read Olivia's total instead of finding the difference

params.description: Emma and Olivia rows highlight.

steps.text: The question asks how many FEWER Emma read than Olivia. You need the difference between them. Find both totals, then subtract.

incorrects.description: Student selected 60: added Olivia and Emma's totals instead of finding the difference

params.description: Emma and Olivia rows highlight.

steps.text: The word FEWER tells us to compare, not add. Find how many books Olivia read, then how many Emma read. Subtract to find the difference.

incorrects.description: Student answered incorrectly. System models the correct answer.

params.description: System highlights Emma's row showing 4 symbols, counts by 5s: 5, 10, 15, 20. Then highlights Olivia's row showing 8 symbols, counts by 5s: 5, 10, 15, 20, 25, 30, 35, 40. Visual shows subtraction: 40 minus 20 equals 20.

steps.text: Let me show you. Emma has 4 symbols. Count by 5s: 5, 10, 15, 20. That's 20 books. Olivia has 8 symbols: 5, 10, 15, 20, 25, 30, 35, 40. That's 40 books. The difference between 40 and 20 is 20. Emma read 20 fewer books than Olivia.

steps.text: Look at the numbers on the axis. They don't go 31, 32, 33. They jump by 5s. What comes after 30? After 35?

incorrects.description: Student selected 30 — went backward instead of forward in the sequence

steps.text: 30 comes before 35, not after. The axis counts up: 0, 5, 10, 15... What's the next jump after 35?

incorrects.description: Student answered incorrectly. System models the correct answer.

params.description: Axis numbers highlight in sequence: 5, 10, 15, 20, 25, 30, 35, 40.

steps.text: Here's how the axis works. It counts by 5s: 5, 10, 15, 20, 25, 30, 35. After 35, add 5 more. That gives us 40. The answer is 40. That's how a scale of 5 works on a bar graph axis.

steps.text: Let's think about this together. The axis counts by 5s: 5, 10, 15, 20, 25, 30, 35, 40, 45. For each day, find the number in the data table, then set the bar to end at that number on the axis. Where does Monday's 40 land?

incorrects.description: Student set one or more bars incorrectly three or more times. System models the answer.

params.description: System demonstrates setting all four bars. Saturday bar extends to 35, Sunday bar extends to 30, Monday bar extends to 40, Tuesday bar extends to 25.

steps.text: Let me show you how this works. Saturday has 35 minutes, so I set the bar to end at 35 on the axis. Sunday has 30, so the bar ends at 30. Monday has 40, the bar ends at 40. Tuesday has 25, the bar ends at 25. The bar length shows the value. Each bar ends right on the axis number that matches the data.

steps.text: You're looking at the scale. We need the total for these two days. What value does Saturday's bar reach?

incorrects.description: Student selected 35, only Saturday's value

params.description: Saturday and Sunday bars pulse to draw attention to both.

steps.text: You found Saturday: 35 minutes. The question asks for Saturday AND Sunday. Find Sunday's value, then add them together.

params.description: Saturday and Sunday bars highlight separately, one after the other.

steps.text: Let's check each bar carefully. Saturday reaches 35 on the axis. Sunday reaches 30. Count by 5s if that helps. What do you get when you add 35 and 30?

incorrects.description: Student answered incorrectly three or more times. System models the answer.

params.description: System traces Saturday bar from 0 to 35, then Sunday bar from 0 to 30, showing the values on axis.

steps.text: Let me show you. Saturday's bar ends at 35. Sunday's bar ends at 30. When we add them, 35 plus 30 equals 65. The answer is 65 minutes in all. We read each bar's height, then add those values together.
